title: euodia nadeaudii
intro: Euodia nadeaudii, also known as the Taiwanese cinnamon or Taiwanese cassia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lauraceae. This plant is native to Taiwan and has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Euodia nadeaudii include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate pain, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Euodia nadeaudii is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to make medicinal compounds like essential oils and extracts.
